canonical_definition: The latent ability of a language model to encode abstract, author-specific patterns such as syntax, diction, and rhetorical structure from text, independent of semantic or factual content.
papers: Text Takes Over: A Study of Modality Bias in Multimodal Intent Detection

definitions.text: The latent ability of a language model to encode abstract, author-specific patterns such as syntax, diction, and rhetorical structure from text, independent of semantic or factual content.
definitions.paper: Text Takes Over: A Study of Modality Bias in Multimodal Intent Detection

definitions.text: The latent ability of a language model to capture and represent stylistic features of an author's writing within prompt embeddings, even without explicit instruction to do so.

characterization: The latent ability of a language model to encode abstract, author-specific patterns such as syntax, diction, and rhetorical structure from text, independent of semantic or factual content.

emnlp25: Text Takes Over: A Study of Modality Bias in Multimodal Intent Detection
